questions.q: Hypotension, jugular venous distension, and muffled heart sounds. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Beck's Triad ➔ Indicates: Cardiac Tamponade (اندحاس قلبي)

questions.q: Stasis of blood flow, endothelial injury, and hypercoagulability. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Virchow's Triad ➔ Indicates: Deep Vein Thrombosis / DVT (جلطة الأوردة العميقة)

questions.q: Nystagmus, intention tremor, and scanning speech. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Charcot's Neurologic Triad ➔ Indicates: Multiple Sclerosis (التصلب المتعدد)

questions.q: Right upper quadrant pain, jaundice, and fever. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Charcot's Cholangitis Triad ➔ Indicates: Acute Cholangitis (التهاب القنوات الصفراوية)

questions.q: Hypertension, bradycardia, and irregular breathing. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Cushing's Triad ➔ Indicates: Increased Intracranial Pressure / ICP (ارتفاع ضغط الدماغ)

questions.q: Symptoms of hypoglycemia, low blood glucose measured, and relief of symptoms after eating. What is the diagnosis?
Polydipsia, polyuria, polyphagia, and unexplained weight loss. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: The 3 Ps ➔ Indicates: Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus

questions.q: Heat intolerance, palpitations, weight loss, and exophthalmos. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Merseburg Triad ➔ Indicates: Graves' Disease (داء غريفز)

questions.q: Ptosis, miosis, and anhidrosis on one side of the face. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Horner's Syndrome ➔ Indicates: Sympathetic trunk lesion

questions.q: Sudden onset of tearing chest pain radiating to the back. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Classic Presentation ➔ Indicates: Aortic Dissection (تسلخ الأبهر)

questions.q: Sudden onset of the 'worst headache of my life' (thunderclap headache). What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Classic Presentation ➔ Indicates: Subarachnoid Hemorrhage (نزيف تحت العنكبوتية)

questions.q: Right upper quadrant pain, jaundice, fever, hypotension, and altered mental status. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Reynold's Pentad ➔ Indicates: Severe Acute Cholangitis

questions.q: Hematuria, flank pain, and a palpable abdominal mass. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Classic Triad ➔ Indicates: Renal Cell Carcinoma (سرطان الخلايا الكلوية)

questions.q: Gait apraxia, dementia, and urinary incontinence (Wobbly, Wacky, Wet). What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Hakim's Triad ➔ Indicates: Normal Pressure Hydrocephalus / NPH

questions.q: Urethritis, conjunctivitis, and arthritis. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Reiter's Triad ➔ Indicates: Reactive Arthritis (التهاب المفاصل التفاعلي)

questions.q: Chronic cough, night sweats, hemoptysis, and weight loss. What is the diagnosis?
Pleuritic chest pain relieved by leaning forward, worsened by lying flat, with a friction rub. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Classic Presentation ➔ Indicates: Acute Pericarditis (التهاب التامور)

questions.q: High fever, chills, and productive cough with rusty sputum. What is the diagnosis?
Microangiopathic hemolytic anemia, thrombocytopenia, and acute kidney injury. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: HUS Triad ➔ Indicates: Hemolytic Uremic Syndrome

questions.q: Situs inversus, chronic sinusitis, and bronchiectasis. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Kartagener Syndrome ➔ Indicates: Primary Ciliary Dyskinesia

questions.q: Fever, anemia, thrombocytopenia, renal failure, and neurologic symptoms. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: FAT RN Pentad ➔ Indicates: Thrombotic Thrombocytopenic Purpura / TTP

questions.q: Painless cervical lymphadenopathy, B symptoms, and Reed-Sternberg cells on biopsy. What is the diagnosis?
Colicky abdominal pain, drawing up legs, and currant jelly stools in an infant. What is the diagnosis?
Projectile non-bilious vomiting and a palpable olive-like abdominal mass in an infant. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Classic Presentation ➔ Indicates: Pyloric Stenosis (تضيق البواب)

questions.q: Palpable purpura on buttocks, arthralgia, and abdominal pain in a child. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Classic Triad ➔ Indicates: Henoch-Schönlein Purpura / IgA Vasculitis

questions.q: Fluctuating muscle weakness that worsens with use, ptosis, and diplopia. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Classic Presentation ➔ Indicates: Myasthenia Gravis (الوهن العضلي الوبيل)

questions.q: Resting tremor, rigidity, bradykinesia, and postural instability. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Parkinsonian Triad ➔ Indicates: Parkinson's Disease (مرض باركنسون)

questions.q: Sudden onset of unilateral facial paralysis including the forehead. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: LMNL Facial Palsy ➔ Indicates: Bell's Palsy (شلل بيل)

questions.q: Painless genital ulcer (chancre) with non-tender lymphadenopathy. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Classic Presentation ➔ Indicates: Primary Syphilis (الزهري الأولي)

questions.q: Painful genital ulcer with exudate and tender inguinal lymphadenopathy. What is the diagnosis?
Cold intolerance, weight gain, fatigue, constipation, and dry skin. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Classic Presentation ➔ Indicates: Hypothyroidism (قصور الغدة الدرقية)

questions.q: Moon facies, buffalo hump, truncal obesity, and purple abdominal striae. What is the diagnosis?
Epistaxis, petechiae, bleeding gums, and circulating blast cells. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Classic Presentation ➔ Indicates: Acute Leukemia (لوكيميا حادة)

questions.q: Bone pain, renal failure, hypercalcemia, and anemia (CRAB). What is the diagnosis?
Hypoxemia, neurologic abnormalities, and a petechial rash following a long bone fracture. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Bergman's Triad ➔ Indicates: Fat Embolism Syndrome (انسداد دهني)

questions.q: Recurrent epistaxis, mucocutaneous telangiectasias, and arteriovenous malformations. What is the diagnosis?
Dysphagia, esophageal webs, and iron deficiency anemia. What is the diagnosis?
Benign ovarian fibroma, ascites, and right-sided pleural effusion. What is the diagnosis?
Gallstones, hiatal hernia, and colonic diverticulosis. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Saint's Triad ➔ Indicates: Often coincidental diseases of Western diet

questions.q: Parathyroid hyperplasia, pancreatic islet cell tumors, and pituitary adenomas (The 3 Ps). What is the diagnosis?
Medullary thyroid carcinoma, pheochromocytoma, and parathyroid hyperplasia. What is the diagnosis?
Medullary thyroid carcinoma, pheochromocytoma, mucosal neuromas, and marfanoid habitus. What is the diagnosis?
Cafe-au-lait macules, polyostotic fibrous dysplasia, and precocious puberty. What is the diagnosis?
Hemolytic anemia, pancytopenia, and venous thrombosis (e.g., Budd-Chiari). What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Classic Triad ➔ Indicates: Paroxysmal Nocturnal Hemoglobinuria (PNH)

questions.q: Rheumatoid arthritis, splenomegaly, and neutropenia. What is the diagnosis?
Dry eyes, dry mouth, and arthritis. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Sicca Syndrome Triad ➔ Indicates: Sjögren's Syndrome

questions.q: Painless jaundice with a palpable, non-tender enlarged gallbladder. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Courvoisier's Sign ➔ Indicates: Pancreatic Head Cancer / Cholangiocarcinoma

questions.q: Abdominal pain, ascites, and hepatomegaly due to hepatic vein thrombosis. What is the diagnosis?
Episodic vertigo, fluctuating tinnitus, and sensorineural hearing loss. What is the diagnosis?
Asthma, aspirin sensitivity, and nasal polyposis. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Samter's Triad ➔ Indicates: Aspirin-Exacerbated Respiratory Disease

questions.q: Eczema, thrombocytopenia, and recurrent pyogenic infections. What is the diagnosis?
Atopic dermatitis, allergic rhinitis, and asthma. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Atopic March Triad ➔ Indicates: Atopy / Allergic tendency

questions.q: Hyperpigmentation, hypotension, hyponatremia, and hyperkalemia. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Classic Presentation ➔ Indicates: Addison's Disease (Primary Adrenal Insufficiency)

questions.q: Episodic headache, sweating, palpitations, and severe hypertension. What is the diagnosis?
Episodic flushing, secretory diarrhea, and right-sided heart valvular lesions. What is the diagnosis?
'Bones, stones, abdominal groans, and psychic moans'. What is the diagnosis?
Dermatitis, diarrhea, and dementia (The 3 Ds). What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Classic Triad ➔ Indicates: Pellagra (Vitamin B3 deficiency)

questions.q: Bleeding gums, easy bruising, corkscrew hairs, and poor wound healing. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Classic Presentation ➔ Indicates: Scurvy (Vitamin C deficiency)

questions.q: Ophthalmoplegia, ataxia, and global confusion. What is the diagnosis?
Confabulation, anterograde amnesia, and retrograde amnesia in a chronic alcoholic. What is the diagnosis?
Chorea, dementia, and behavioral/personality changes. What is the diagnosis?
Optic neuritis, internuclear ophthalmoplegia, and an electric shock sensation radiating down the spine on neck flexion. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Charcot's Triad + Lhermitte's sign ➔ Indicates: Multiple Sclerosis

questions.q: Ascending flaccid paralysis with areflexia following a GI or respiratory infection. What is the diagnosis?
Cherry-red spot on macula, exaggerated startle response, but NO hepatosplenomegaly. What is the diagnosis?
Cherry-red spot on macula WITH massive hepatosplenomegaly. What is the diagnosis?
Bone pain, Erlenmeyer flask deformity of distal femur, and tissue paper macrophages. What is the diagnosis?
Angiokeratomas, peripheral neuropathy, cardiovascular, and renal disease. What is the diagnosis?
Conductive hearing loss, ipsilateral palatal paralysis, and trigeminal neuralgia. What is the diagnosis?
Severe epigastric pain, retching without vomiting, and inability to pass a nasogastric tube. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Borchardt's Triad ➔ Indicates: Gastric Volvulus (التواء المعدة)

questions.q: Vomiting, lower chest pain, and subcutaneous emphysema. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Mackler's Triad ➔ Indicates: Boerhaave Syndrome (تمزق المريء)

questions.q: Upper GI bleeding, jaundice, and biliary colic. What is the diagnosis?
Pneumobilia, small bowel obstruction, and ectopic gallstone seen on X-ray. What is the diagnosis?
Cardiac myxomas, endocrine overactivity, and spotty skin pigmentation. What is the diagnosis?
Aortic stenosis combined with gastrointestinal bleeding from angiodysplasia. What is the diagnosis?
Claudication of buttocks/thighs, absent femoral pulses, and erectile dysfunction. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Leriche Syndrome Triad ➔ Indicates: Aortoiliac occlusive disease

questions.q: Obstructive jaundice caused by a gallstone impacted in the cystic duct extrinsically compressing the common hepatic duct. What is the diagnosis?
Gastric outlet obstruction caused by a large gallstone impacted in the duodenum via a cholecystoduodenal fistula. What is the diagnosis?
Asymptomatic interposition of the colon between the liver and the right hemidiaphragm seen on X-ray. What is the diagnosis?
Perihepatitis with 'violin string' adhesions to the abdominal wall associated with pelvic inflammatory disease. What is the diagnosis?
Chemical pneumonitis caused by aspiration of gastric contents during general anesthesia. What is the diagnosis?
Hoarseness due to left recurrent laryngeal nerve palsy caused by left atrial enlargement. What is the diagnosis?
Dyspnea and hypoxemia that worsen when sitting upright and improve when lying flat. What is the diagnosis?
Compression of the left common iliac vein by the right common iliac artery leading to left leg DVT. What is the diagnosis?
Effort-induced thrombosis of the axillary or subclavian vein in young athletes. What is the diagnosis?
Thrombophlebitis of the internal jugular vein following an oropharyngeal infection. What is the diagnosis?
Otitis media, severe facial pain in trigeminal distribution, and ipsilateral abducens nerve palsy. What is the diagnosis?
Paralysis of cranial nerves IX, X, and XI due to a lesion at the jugular foramen. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Jugular Foramen Syndrome ➔ Indicates: Vernet's Syndrome

questions.q: Paralysis of cranial nerves IX, X, XI, and XII WITHOUT sympathetic involvement. What is the diagnosis?
Paralysis of cranial nerves IX, X, XI, and XII WITH ipsilateral Horner's syndrome. What is the diagnosis?
Loss of pain/temperature on the ipsilateral face and contralateral body, dysphagia, and Horner's syndrome. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Lateral Medullary Syndrome ➔ Indicates: Wallenberg Syndrome

questions.q: Ipsilateral oculomotor nerve palsy and contralateral hemiparesis. What is the diagnosis?
Ipsilateral oculomotor nerve palsy with contralateral ataxia and tremor. What is the diagnosis?
Upward gaze palsy, pupillary light-near dissociation, and convergence-retraction nystagmus. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Dorsal Midbrain Syndrome ➔ Indicates: Parinaud's Syndrome

questions.q: Ipsilateral anosmia, ipsilateral optic atrophy, and contralateral papilledema. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Classic Triad ➔ Indicates: Foster Kennedy Syndrome

questions.q: Cortical blindness with the patient confabulating visual images and denying their visual loss (anosognosia). What is the diagnosis?
Agraphia, acalculia, finger agnosia, and left-right disorientation. What is the diagnosis?
Complex visual hallucinations in psychologically normal patients with severe visual impairment. What is the diagnosis?
questions.a: Classic Presentation ➔ Indicates: Charles Bonnet Syndrome

questions.q: Giant hemangioma accompanied by profound thrombocytopenia and consumptive coagulopathy. What is the diagnosis?
Port-wine stain, varicose veins, and bony/soft tissue hypertrophy of an extremity. What is the diagnosis?
Fibrofolliculomas, pulmonary cysts with spontaneous pneumothorax, and renal tumors. What is the diagnosis?
